title: Bolad

'''Bolad''' was a terrestrial astronomical object located in the [[Bolad system/Legends|Bolad system]], a part of the [[Tharin sector/Legends|Tharin sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="EAOC" /> The [[Annoo-dat Blue]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Sise Fromm]],<ref name="Polyhedron170" /> based out of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Annoo (Gelefil)|Annoo]],<ref name="CSWE Sise">{{CSWECite|I|297|Fromm, Sise}}</ref> maintained a [[Sise Fromm's vacation palace|vacation palace]] on Bolad until the residence was destroyed by Fromm's [[Gender/Legends|son]], [[Tig Fromm|Tig]],<ref name="Polyhedron170" /> at some point by [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]].<ref name="15 BBY">{{PolyhedronCite|170|Star Wars Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO}} establishes that the [[Star Wars: Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO|''Star Wars: Droids'']] animated series, by the [[Time/Legends|time]] of which [[Tig Fromm]] has destroyed [[Sise Fromm's vacation palace]] on Bolad, is set fifteen [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before the events of {{Film|IV}}, which corresponds to [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]] per ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Bolad was first mentioned in "[[Star Wars Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO]]," a [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] source article authored by [[Rich Handley]] and [[Abel G. Peña]] for use with the [[Wizards of the Coast]] ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]'' and published in the [[Polyhedron 170|170th issue]] of the ''[[Polyhedron]]'' magazine<ref name="Polyhedron170" /> by [[June 3]], [[2004]].<ref name="Polyhedron170date">{{SWArchive|url=gaming/other/rpg/news20040603.html|text=Droids in ''Polyhedron''}}</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Bolad [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the celestial body itself, in grid square S-8.<ref name="TEA" />
